behaviors.description: A request whose signature does not match its body is rejected with a 401 response; the body is not forwarded.
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary is handler-to-queue handoff: reject + do-not-forward must be verified together. A unit test of only HMAC logic can miss accidental enqueue on failure. Rejected higher levels: behavior text is about header/body validation and forwarding control, not real network/subprocess/OS effects or any AI output.
behaviors.test_description: Run the HTTP handler in-process with real signature verification code and a spy/fake queue module. Send a request with mismatched `X-Signature-256` vs raw body; assert `401` and assert queue `enqueue` was not called.

behaviors.description: An event is written to the persistent queue before the HTTP 200 acknowledgment is returned to the sender.
behaviors.justification: This is an ordering guarantee across modules (HTTP handler + persistent queue adapter). Mocking persistence can hide real ordering bugs, so use the real local queue store. Rejected System: behavior does not require real external HTTP or subprocess; in-process server + real local persistence is sufficient.
behaviors.test_description: Use in-process HTTP invocation wired to the real persistent queue implementation (e.g., local DB/file-backed queue). Inject a controlled delay/failure point around queue write completion and assert response `200` is emitted only after successful write commit.

behaviors.description: The handler returns the HTTP response without waiting for downstream processing of the event to finish.
behaviors.justification: Failure appears at boundary between request handler, queue write, and async worker trigger. Unit logic alone may miss accidental await/join on worker completion. Rejected System: requirement is about non-blocking orchestration, not real network stack behavior.
behaviors.test_description: Wire handler with a downstream processor stub that blocks for a long time. Send a valid request and assert HTTP response returns quickly while processor promise is still unresolved; then resolve processor and confirm no impact on already-sent response.

behaviors.description: The response arrives within 3 seconds wall-clock time even when downstream processing of earlier events is slow and concurrent.
behaviors.justification: This is a real-time SLA under concurrency; failures often depend on event-loop scheduling, socket handling, and runtime contention that mocks can hide. Real wall-clock and concurrent request execution are part of the behavior text. Rejected Integration: text explicitly requires 'within 3 seconds wall-clock' under 'slow and concurrent' load, which is an OS/runtime boundary risk.
behaviors.test_description: Start the service process with real HTTP listener. Create concurrent traffic where earlier events trigger slow downstream work, then send new valid webhook requests and measure end-to-end latency over actual sockets; assert responses are consistently under 3 seconds.

behaviors.description: An event whose ID was already received is not written to the queue a second time.
behaviors.justification: Dedup correctness is a state-handoff concern between handler and persistent dedup/queue store. A pure unit test can miss duplicate writes caused by repository/query wiring. Rejected System: no requirement for real network/subprocess; can be reproduced with in-process modules and local persistence.
behaviors.test_description: Use real dedup and queue persistence modules in a local test store. Send two valid requests with the same event ID; assert first write succeeds, second is acknowledged per contract but does not create a second queue record.

behaviors.description: When the queue reports it is full, the handler returns 503 with a Retry-After header.
behaviors.justification: Behavior is contract mapping across modules: queue-capacity signal to HTTP status/headers. Mocking at too high a level can miss mapping bugs, but real OS/network is not required. Rejected System: behavior text is about handler response mapping, not socket-level/network failure modes.
behaviors.test_description: Wire handler to queue module configured to return `full` on enqueue attempt. Send valid webhook request and assert HTTP `503` plus `Retry-After` header is present and correctly formatted.

behaviors.description: The persistent queue actually enforces its configured capacity limit and rejects events that would exceed it.
behaviors.justification: This validates real queue persistence behavior (capacity enforcement) and should use the real storage-backed queue module, not a stub. Still below System because no external API/subprocess/real network is required by the behavior text. Rejected System: 'persistent queue enforces configured capacity' can be validated with local module + local DB/filesystem.
behaviors.test_description: Instantiate the real persistent queue with small configured capacity in a local test database/filesystem. Enqueue up to limit, then enqueue one more and assert explicit rejection/error path with no overflow record persisted.
